hero_subtitle: SFTY empowers government teams to connect fragmented market data into structured supervisory workflows with evidence-grade logging and multi-provider orchestration.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Supervisory visibility breaks across disconnected data
problem_description: Regulators struggle to form a clear market view when licensing, transaction, identity and Travel Rule data remain siloed across separate systems. This fragmentation slows supervisory review and weakens the ability to trace risk signals and defend oversight decisions.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects licensing, transaction, identity, Travel Rule data and risk signals into a unified supervisory operating layer that enables regulated authorities to trace, review and document oversight activities efficiently.
workflow_title: From market signal to supervisory action
workflow_description: SFTY connects fragmented market signals, VASP and wallet data into structured alerts, cases, evidence and supervisory actions for regulatory teams.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. One supervisory layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects fragmented market data, licensing, wallet activity, cases and reporting into a secure supervisory layer designed for institutional oversight.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY provides public authorities with a secure, auditable platform supporting controlled access, evidence-grade logging and procurement-ready deployment in regulated supervisory environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support supervisory visibility of digital assets
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how supervisory cases connect wallets and entity data
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports audit-ready reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe secure deployment options for government supervisory teams
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for regulators overseeing digital assets?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides regulators a secure supervisory layer connecting VASP data, wallets, transactions, licensing records, KYT, Travel Rule, cases and reports for market-level digital asset oversight.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY support VASP supervisory workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY supports VASP workflows by linking licensing data, wallet activity, Travel Rule messages and alerts into structured cases and supervisory actions within a controlled government environment.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve supervisory evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves audit-ready evidence by recording decision logs, case histories, alerts and supporting data with access governance and evidence-grade logging for supervisory review.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY supports modular integration with government registries, reporting systems and multiple provider outputs under controlled deployment and role-based access governance.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace analytic or compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY sits above analytics and compliance tools without replacing them, orchestrating provider outputs into structured supervisory workflows and audit-ready case management.
final_title: Review supervisory workflows and market visibility
final_description: Explore how SFTY connects digital asset data, cases and evidence into a single supervisory operating layer for regulators.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
